docs.title: ai-rulez ⚡
docs.text: Lightning-fast CLI tool and MCP server for managing AI assistant rules across Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, Copilot, and other AI tools from a single YAML configuration.

docs.title: What is ai-rulez?
docs.text: ai-rulez generates AI assistant instruction files from a single configuration. Instead of maintaining separate .cursorrules, CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md files, you write one ai_rulez.yaml and generate all formats.

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
Create an ai_rulez.yaml:

metadata:
  name: "My Project"

rules:
  - name: "code-style"
    content: "Use TypeScript with strict mode"
    priority: 10

outputs:
  - path: "CLAUDE.md"
    template: "claude"
  - path: ".cursor/rules/"
    template: "cursor"

Run ai-rulez generate and get both files automatically generated and kept in sync.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Single Source of Truth - One YAML file generates all AI assistant formats
  • Multiple AI Platforms - Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, Copilot, Gemini, and more
  • MCP Server - Built-in Model Context Protocol server for dynamic configuration
  • Git Hooks Integration - Automatic validation with lefthook, pre-commit, or husky
  • Remote Includes - Share configurations across projects
  • Agent Support - Specialized sub-agents for different tasks

docs.title: Supported AI Platforms
docs.text: Platform Output Format Claude CLAUDE.md Markdown Claude Agents .claude/agents/ Directory with specialized agents Cursor .cursor/rules/ MDC files Windsurf .windsurf/ Markdown files GitHub Copilot .github/copilot-instructions.md Markdown Gemini GEMINI.md Markdown Cline .clinerules/ Numbered markdown files Continue.dev .continue/rules/ Frontmatter markdown AMP/Codex AGENTS.md Shared format

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
HomebrewGonpmpipRun without installing 
brew install goldziher/tap/ai-rulez

go install github.com/Goldziher/ai-rulez/cmd@latest

npm install -g ai-rulez

pip install ai-rulez

# Go
go run github.com/Goldziher/ai-rulez/cmd@latest --help

# Python
uvx ai-rulez --help

# Node.js
npx ai-rulez@latest --help

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
1. Install ai-rulez
  2. Follow the Quick Start guide
  3. Learn about Configuration
  4. Browse Examples

docs.title: init
docs.text:
Create a new project configuration.

# Simple start - creates Claude configuration
ai-rulez init "My Project"

# Popular AI platforms  
ai-rulez init "My Project" --popular

# Specific platform
ai-rulez init "My Project" --preset cursor

docs.title: generate
docs.text:
Generate AI assistant files.

# Generate all configured outputs
ai-rulez generate

# Preview without writing files
ai-rulez generate --dry-run

docs.title: validate
docs.text:
Check configuration syntax.

ai-rulez validate

docs.title: list
docs.text:
Display configuration elements.

# List all rules
ai-rulez list rules

# List with details
ai-rulez list rules --detailed

# Filter by name
ai-rulez list rules --filter "testing"

# JSON output
ai-rulez list rules --json

Available list commands: rules, sections, agents, outputs

docs.title: Provider Selection
docs.text:
# All supported platforms
ai-rulez init "My Project" --all

# Specific combinations
ai-rulez init "My Project" --claude --cursor --copilot

# Available platforms:
--claude --cursor --windsurf --copilot --gemini --amp --codex --cline --continue-dev

docs.title: AI Agent Support
docs.text:
# Use AI to generate configuration
ai-rulez init "My Project" --use-agent claude

# Skip AI assistance  
ai-rulez init "My Project" --no-agent

# List available AI agents
ai-rulez init --list-agents

docs.title: generate
docs.text:
Generate AI assistant files from configuration.

ai-rulez generate [flags]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Generate all outputs
ai-rulez generate

# Use specific config file
ai-rulez generate --config custom.yaml

# Dry run (preview without writing)
ai-rulez generate --dry-run

# Generate recursively (find all config files)
ai-rulez generate --recursive

docs.title: validate
docs.text:
Validate configuration syntax and structure.

ai-rulez validate [flags]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Validate current directory config
ai-rulez validate

# Validate specific file
ai-rulez validate --config custom.yaml

# Validate recursively
ai-rulez validate --recursive

docs.title: Configuration Management
docs.text: Manage your configuration without editing files.

docs.title: Managing Rules
docs.text:
# Add a simple rule
ai-rulez add rule "testing" --content "Write unit tests"

# Add rule with priority
ai-rulez add rule "security" --content "Validate all inputs" --priority 10

# Update rule content
ai-rulez update rule "testing" --content "Write comprehensive unit tests" 

# Remove rule
ai-rulez delete rule "testing"

docs.title: Managing Outputs
docs.text:
# Add output for new AI platform
ai-rulez add output --path "GEMINI.md"

# Add directory output
ai-rulez add output --path ".windsurf/" --type "rule" --naming-scheme "rules.md"

# Remove output
ai-rulez delete output --path "GEMINI.md"

docs.title: Advanced: Sections & Agents
docs.text:
# Add documentation section
ai-rulez add section "Architecture" --content "Clean architecture with DDD"

# Add Claude agent
ai-rulez add agent "reviewer" \
  --description "Code reviewer" \
  --system-prompt "You are a senior developer" \
  --tools "read,edit"

docs.title: mcp
docs.text:
Start MCP server for AI assistant integration.

ai-rulez mcp [flags]

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Start MCP server (stdio transport)
ai-rulez mcp

# Use custom config
ai-rulez mcp --config custom.yaml

# Enable debug logging
ai-rulez mcp --debug

See MCP Server documentation for integration details.

docs.title: Global Flags
docs.text:
--config string    Config file path (default: auto-detect)
--debug           Enable debug output with detailed logging
--verbose         Enable verbose output for operations  
--quiet, -q       Suppress progress bars and non-essential output
--help, -h        Show help for any command
--version         Show version number

docs.title: completion
docs.text:
Generate shell completion scripts:

# Bash
ai-rulez completion bash

# Zsh  
ai-rulez completion zsh

# Fish
ai-rulez completion fish

# PowerShell
ai-rulez completion powershell

docs.title: help
docs.text:
Get help for any command:

ai-rulez help
ai-rulez help init
ai-rulez help add rule

docs.title: Configuration File Detection
docs.text:
ai-rulez looks for configuration files in this order:

  1. --config flag value
  2. ai_rulez.yaml
  3. ai-rulez.yaml
  4. .ai_rulez.yaml
  5. .ai-rulez.yaml
  6. Same files with .yml extension

Searches current directory, then parent directories recursively.

docs.title: Basic Structure
docs.text: Start with the minimal configuration, then add complexity as needed.

docs.title: Minimal Configuration
docs.text:
metadata:
  name: "My Project"

rules:
  - name: "code-quality"
    content: "Write clean, readable code"

outputs:
  - path: "CLAUDE.md"

docs.title: Add Priorities
docs.text:
Control the order of rules in generated files:

metadata:
  name: "My Project"

rules:
  - name: "security"
    content: "Validate all inputs"
    priority: 10          # Higher = appears first

  - name: "code-quality"
    content: "Write clean, readable code"
    priority: 5

outputs:
  - path: "CLAUDE.md"

docs.title: Multiple AI Platforms
docs.text:
Generate files for different AI tools:

metadata:
  name: "My Project"

rules:
  - name: "code-quality"
    content: "Write clean, readable code"
    priority: 10

outputs:
  - path: "CLAUDE.md"
  - path: ".cursor/rules/rules.mdc"
  - path: ".github/copilot-instructions.md"

docs.title: Complete Structure
docs.text:
All available configuration options:

metadata:
  name: "My Project"
  description: "Optional description"
  version: "1.0.0"

rules:
  - name: "rule-name"
    content: "Rule description"
    priority: 10

sections:
  - title: "Section Title" 
    content: "Longer documentation content"
    priority: 5

agents:
  - name: "agent-name"
    description: "Agent purpose"
    system_prompt: "You are a..."
    tools: ["read", "write"]
    priority: 8

outputs:
  - path: "CLAUDE.md"

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
Individual instructions for AI assistants:

rules:
  - name: "typescript-strict"
    content: "Use TypeScript strict mode with explicit return types"
    priority: 10              # Higher = appears first

docs.title: Sections
docs.text:
Longer documentation blocks:

sections:
  - title: "Project Setup"
    content: |
      This project uses:
      - TypeScript 5.0+
      - React 18
    priority: 10

docs.title: Agents (Claude Only)
docs.text:
Specialized sub-agents for specific tasks:

agents:
  - name: "code-reviewer"
    description: "Reviews code for best practices"
    system_prompt: "You are a senior code reviewer focused on quality"
    tools: ["read", "grep", "edit"]
    priority: 10

Available Tools: read, write, edit, grep, bash, multiedit

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text:
Define which files to generate:

outputs:
  # Single files (most common)
  - path: "CLAUDE.md"
  - path: ".cursor/rules/rules.mdc"
  - path: ".github/copilot-instructions.md"

  # Directory outputs  
  - path: ".windsurf/"
    type: "rule"
    naming_scheme: "{name}.md"           # One file per rule

  - path: ".claude/agents/"
    type: "agent" 
    naming_scheme: "{name}.md"           # One file per agent

Output Field Options:

  • path: File or directory path (recommended)
  • file: ⚠️ DEPRECATED - Use path instead. Will be removed in v2.0
  • type: Content type for directory outputs (rule, section, agent)
  • naming_scheme: File naming pattern for directory outputs
  • template: Custom template (optional - uses default if not specified)

Available Templates:

  • default: Standard markdown template (used when no template specified)
  • Inline templates: Define custom templates directly in configuration:
outputs:
  - path: "CUSTOM.md"
    template: |
      # {{.ProjectName}}
      {{range .Rules}}
      ## {{.Name}} (Priority: {{.Priority}})
      {{.Content}}
      {{end}}

docs.title: Remote Includes
docs.text:
Share configurations across projects:

includes:
  - "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/myorg/standards/main/backend.yaml"

rules:
  - name: "project-specific"
    content: "This rule is specific to this project"

Remote rules are merged with local ones. Local rules override remote rules with the same name.

docs.title: User-Specific Rules
docs.text:
Override rules for specific users or environments:

user_rulez:
  rules:
    - name: "dev-specific"
      content: "Development-only rule"
      priority: 10
  sections:
    - title: "Dev Environment"
      content: "Local development setup"
  agents:
    - name: "dev-helper"
      description: "Development assistant"

docs.title: Targets (Advanced)
docs.text:
Target specific file patterns or directories:

targets:
  backend: ["src/**/*.go", "internal/**/*.go"]
  frontend: ["web/**/*.ts", "web/**/*.tsx"]

Note: This is an advanced feature - most users should use the standard outputs configuration.

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
ai-rulez validate

See the Examples page for complete configurations.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: Start with the simplest setup and progressively build more complex configurations.

docs.title: 1. Minimal Setup
docs.text:
The absolute simplest configuration - one file, one rule:

metadata:
  name: "My Project"

rules:
  - name: "code-quality"
    content: "Write clean, readable code"

outputs:
  - path: "CLAUDE.md"

ai-rulez generate  # Creates CLAUDE.md

docs.title: 2. Add Multiple Rules
docs.text:
Add priorities to control ordering:

metadata:
  name: "My Project"

rules:
  - name: "typescript-strict"
    content: "Use TypeScript strict mode with explicit types" 
    priority: 10

  - name: "testing"
    content: "Write unit tests for all functions"
    priority: 8

outputs:
  - path: "CLAUDE.md"

docs.title: 3. Multiple AI Platforms
docs.text:
Generate files for different AI tools:

metadata:
  name: "My Project"

rules:
  - name: "code-quality"
    content: "Write clean, readable code"
    priority: 10

outputs:
  - path: "CLAUDE.md"
  - path: ".cursor/rules/rules.mdc"
  - path: ".github/copilot-instructions.md"

Or use presets for quick setup: 

ai-rulez init "My Project" --popular  # Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, Copilot

docs.title: 4. Add Documentation Sections
docs.text:
Include longer documentation blocks:

metadata:
  name: "TypeScript App"

rules:
  - name: "typescript-strict"
    content: "Use TypeScript strict mode with explicit types"
    priority: 10

sections:
  - title: "Project Setup"
    content: |
      This project uses:
      - TypeScript 5.0+
      - React 18
      - Vite for bundling
    priority: 10

outputs:
  - path: "CLAUDE.md"

docs.title: 5. Claude Agents
docs.text:
Add specialized sub-agents for specific tasks:

metadata:
  name: "Enterprise App"

rules:
  - name: "code-quality"
    content: "Write clean, maintainable code"
    priority: 10

agents:
  - name: "code-reviewer"
    description: "Reviews code for best practices"
    system_prompt: "You are a senior code reviewer focused on quality and security"
    tools: ["read", "grep", "edit"]
    priority: 10

outputs:
  - path: "CLAUDE.md"
  - path: ".claude/agents/"
    type: "agent"
    naming_scheme: "{name}.md"

docs.title: 6. Remote Configuration Sharing
docs.text:
Share common rules across projects:

includes:
  - "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/myorg/standards/main/backend.yaml"

metadata:
  name: "User Service"

rules:
  - name: "service-specific"
    content: "Handle user authentication with JWT"
    priority: 10

outputs:
  - path: "CLAUDE.md"

Remote rules are merged with local ones. Local rules override remote rules with the same name.

docs.title: Language-Specific Examples
docs.text: Build on the foundation above with these complete project examples.

docs.title: Python Data Science Project
docs.text:
metadata:
  name: "ML Pipeline"

rules:
  - name: "python-version"
    content: "Use Python 3.11+ with type hints"
    priority: 10

  - name: "data-processing" 
    content: "Use pandas for data, numpy for numerical computing"
    priority: 9

  - name: "testing"
    content: "Write tests with pytest, use fixtures for data setup"
    priority: 8

sections:
  - title: "Development Environment"
    content: |
      - Use conda for environment management
      - Pin dependency versions in requirements.txt
      - Use Jupyter notebooks for experimentation only
    priority: 10

outputs:
  - path: "CLAUDE.md"
  - path: ".cursor/rules/rules.mdc"

docs.title: React Frontend Application
docs.text:
metadata:
  name: "React Dashboard"

rules:
  - name: "react-patterns"
    content: "Use functional components with hooks, implement error boundaries"
    priority: 10

  - name: "styling"
    content: "Use Tailwind CSS with design system tokens"
    priority: 9

sections:
  - title: "Project Structure"
    content: |
      ```
      src/
      ├── components/     # Reusable UI components  
      ├── pages/         # Page components
      ├── hooks/         # Custom hooks
      └── services/      # API calls
      ```
    priority: 10

outputs:
  - path: "CLAUDE.md"
  - path: ".cursor/rules/rules.mdc"
  - path: ".windsurf/rules.md"

docs.title: Go CLI Tool
docs.text:
metadata:
  name: "CLI Tool"

rules:
  - name: "cli-design"
    content: "Use cobra for commands, provide helpful error messages"
    priority: 10

  - name: "user-experience"
    content: "Add progress bars, use colors with --no-color flag"
    priority: 9

outputs:
  - path: "CLAUDE.md"
  - path: "AGENTS.md"  # For AMP/Codex

Start with the minimal examples above, then customize for your specific needs.

docs.title: Package Managers
docs.text:
Homebrew (macOS/Linux)Gonpmpip 
brew install goldziher/tap/ai-rulez

go install github.com/Goldziher/ai-rulez/cmd@latest

npm install -g ai-rulez

docs.title: Run Without Installing
docs.text:
GoPythonNode.js 
go run github.com/Goldziher/ai-rulez/cmd@latest --help

uvx ai-rulez --help

docs.title: Shell Completions (Optional)
docs.text:
Enable tab completion for your shell for faster command entry:

BashZshFishPowerShell 
# Add to ~/.bashrc or ~/.bash_profile
source <(ai-rulez completion bash)

# Or install permanently
ai-rulez completion bash > /etc/bash_completion.d/ai-rulez

# Add to ~/.zshrc
source <(ai-rulez completion zsh)

# Or for oh-my-zsh
ai-rulez completion zsh > ~/.oh-my-zsh/completions/_ai-rulez

ai-rulez completion fish | source

# Or install permanently
ai-rulez completion fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/ai-rulez.fish

# Add to PowerShell profile
ai-rulez completion powershell | Out-String | Invoke-Expression

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
ai-rulez --version

Test completion works: 

ai-rulez <TAB><TAB>  # Should show available commands

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: After installation, start with the Quick Start guide.

docs.title: MCP Server
docs.text: ai-rulez includes a built-in MCP server for dynamic configuration management with AI assistants.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: ai-rulez mcp

docs.title: Claude Desktop
docs.text:
Add to ~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "ai-rulez": {
      "command": "ai-rulez",
      "args": ["mcp"]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Claude Code
docs.text: claude mcp add ai-rulez "ai-rulez" "mcp"

docs.title: Via Package Managers
docs.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "ai-rulez": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "ai-rulez@latest", "mcp"]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Available Tools
docs.text: The MCP server provides 19 tools for managing configuration:

docs.title: Configuration Retrieval
docs.text:
• get_rules - List all rules (supports min_priority, name_filter parameters)
  • get_sections - List all sections (supports name filter parameter)
  • get_agents - List all agents (supports name filter parameter)
  • get_outputs - List all outputs (supports path filter parameter)

docs.title: Add Elements
docs.text:
• add_rule - Add new rule (content required, section, priority optional)
  • add_section - Add new section (name required, priority optional)
  • add_agent - Add new agent (name, description required, priority, tools, system_prompt optional)
  • add_output - Add new output (path required, type, naming_scheme optional)

docs.title: Update Elements (Index-Based)
docs.text:
• update_rule - Update rule by index (index, content required, priority optional)
  • update_section - Update section by index (index, name required, priority optional)
  • update_agent - Update agent by index (index required, all other fields optional)
  • update_output - Update output by index (index required, all other fields optional)

docs.title: Delete Elements (Index-Based)
docs.text:
• delete_rule - Delete rule by index (index required)
  • delete_section - Delete section by index (index required)
  • delete_agent - Delete agent by index (index required)
  • delete_output - Delete output by index (index required)

docs.title: Project Operations
docs.text:
• generate_output - Generate files (config_file optional)
  • validate_config - Validate configuration (config_file required)
  • init_project - Initialize project (name required, preset, use_agent optional)
  • get_version - Get ai-rulez version (no parameters)

docs.title: Usage Examples
docs.text:
Simply ask your AI assistant to manage configurations:

"Add a rule with content 'Write unit tests' and priority 8"

"Update rule at index 0 with new content 'Write comprehensive unit tests'"

"Generate the AI files from my configuration"

"Get all rules with priority higher than 5"

"Initialize a new project called 'My API' with preset claude"

Note: Update and delete operations use zero-based indices. Use get_* tools first to find the correct index.

docs.title: Benefits
docs.text:
• Dynamic Updates - No file editing required
  • Real-time Generation - Immediate file generation after changes
  • Automatic Validation - All changes are validated
  • Seamless Integration - Natural AI assistant workflow

docs.title: Custom Configuration
docs.text: ai-rulez mcp --config custom.yaml

docs.title: Monorepo Configuration
docs.text: ai-rulez is designed with monorepos in mind, allowing you to create multiple scoped configurations that reduce context size and improve AI assistant focus.

docs.title: Why Multiple Configurations?
docs.text:
In large codebases, a single massive rules file can overwhelm AI assistants with irrelevant context. Multiple configurations allow you to:

✅ Reduce Context Size - Each part of the codebase gets relevant rules only ✅ Improve Focus - Frontend rules stay with frontend, backend rules with backend ✅ Technology-Specific Guidance - Different stacks get different patterns ✅ Better Performance - Smaller files = faster processing ✅ Easier Maintenance - Update specific areas without affecting others

docs.title: Pattern 1: Technology-Based Separation
docs.text:
Separate configurations by technology stack (most common):

my-project/
├── ai-rulez.yaml           # Root: General project rules + agents
├── backend/
│   └── ai-rulez.yaml       # Backend: API patterns, database rules
└── apps/
    └── ai-rulez.yaml       # Frontend: Component patterns, routing

Root configuration (ai-rulez.yaml): 

metadata:
  name: "MyProject"
  description: "Full-stack application - System Overview"

# Overall architecture and agent definitions
agents:
  - name: "fullstack-architect"
    description: "System architecture and design decisions"
    system_prompt: |
      You are a senior architect. For domain-specific work, delegate to:
      - backend-engineer: API and database tasks
      - frontend-engineer: Component and UI tasks
      - devsecops-engineer: Infrastructure and security

rules:
  - name: "Project Structure"
    priority: 100
    content: |
      **Monorepo Structure**:
      - `backend/` - Fastify API, PostgreSQL, Docker
      - `apps/` - Next.js applications, React components
      - `packages/` - Shared utilities, UI components

outputs:
  - file: "CLAUDE.md"
  - path: ".cursor/rules/"
    type: "rule"
    naming_scheme: "project-rules.mdc"

Backend configuration (backend/ai-rulez.yaml): 

metadata:
  name: "MyProject Backend"
  description: "API Server - Patterns and Guidelines"

rules:
  - name: "API Patterns"
    priority: 80
    content: |
      **Route Pattern**:
      ```typescript
      app.post<{ Body: CreateInput }>('/items', {
        preHandler: app.auth([app.requireRole(Role.Admin)]),
        schema: { body: CreateSchema }
      }, async (request) => {
        const item = await createItem(request.body);
        return { data: item };
      });
      ```

  - name: "Database Operations"
    priority: 75
    content: |
      - Use transactions for multi-table operations
      - Handle unique constraints gracefully
      - Use typed queries with proper error handling

outputs:
  - file: "CLAUDE.md"
  - file: ".cursor/rules/backend-rules.mdc"

Frontend configuration (apps/ai-rulez.yaml): 

metadata:
  name: "MyProject Frontend"
  description: "Next.js Applications - Patterns and Guidelines"

rules:
  - name: "Component Patterns"
    priority: 80
    content: |
      **Component Structure**:
      ```typescript
      'use client';

      export function ItemCard({ item }: { item: Item }) {
        const { mutate } = useSWR(`/api/items/${item.id}`);
        return <div>...</div>;
      }
      ```

  - name: "State Management"
    priority: 75
    content: |
      - Use Zustand for client state
      - Use SWR for server state
      - Prefer server components when possible

outputs:
  - file: "CLAUDE.md"
  - file: ".cursor/rules/frontend-rules.mdc"

docs.title: Pattern 2: Service-Based Separation
docs.text:
For microservices architectures:

project/
├── ai-rulez.yaml           # Root: Overall architecture
├── services/
│   ├── auth-service/
│   │   └── ai-rulez.yaml   # Authentication patterns
│   ├── payment-service/
│   │   └── ai-rulez.yaml   # Payment processing rules
│   └── notification-service/
│       └── ai-rulez.yaml   # Notification patterns
└── shared/
    └── ai-rulez.yaml       # Shared utilities and types

docs.title: Pattern 3: Feature-Based Separation
docs.text:
For feature-driven development:

app/
├── ai-rulez.yaml           # Root: App-wide patterns
├── features/
│   ├── auth/
│   │   └── ai-rulez.yaml   # Authentication feature rules
│   ├── dashboard/
│   │   └── ai-rulez.yaml   # Dashboard patterns
│   └── settings/
│       └── ai-rulez.yaml   # Settings UI patterns
└── shared/
    └── ai-rulez.yaml       # Shared components and utils

docs.title: 1. Root Configuration Strategy
docs.text:
Your root ai-rulez.yaml should contain:

  • System-wide architecture rules
  • Agent definitions with delegation patterns
  • Technology stack overview
  • Cross-cutting concerns (logging, error handling)
# Root ai-rulez.yaml
metadata:
  name: "Project Name"
  description: "System overview and architecture"

agents:
  - name: "system-architect"
    description: "Overall system design and cross-cutting concerns"
    system_prompt: |
      You are the system architect. When working on specific domains:
      - Use backend-engineer agent for API/database work
      - Use frontend-engineer agent for UI/component work
      - Use devsecops-engineer agent for infrastructure

rules:
  - name: "Architecture Overview" 
    priority: 100
    content: |
      **System Architecture**: Microservices with React frontend
      **Key Patterns**: Repository pattern, CQRS, Event sourcing
      **Technology**: Node.js, PostgreSQL, Redis, Docker

  - name: "Cross-Cutting Concerns"
    priority: 90
    content: |
      - All services use structured logging with correlation IDs
      - Error handling follows standard HTTP status codes
      - Authentication via JWT with refresh tokens

docs.title: 2. Scoped Configuration Strategy
docs.text:
Each scoped configuration should:

  • Focus on specific technology/domain
  • Include concrete code patterns
  • Reference shared conventions from root
  • Use appropriate priorities (lower than root)
# backend/ai-rulez.yaml
metadata:
  name: "Backend Services"
  description: "API patterns and database operations"

rules:
  - name: "API Route Pattern"
    priority: 80  # Lower than root rules
    content: |
      **Standard Route**:
      ```javascript
      app.post('/api/users', validateRequest, async (req, res) => {
        const user = await userService.create(req.body);
        res.status(201).json({ data: user });
      });
      ```

docs.title: 3. Agent Delegation Patterns
docs.text:
Use agents to route work to the right expertise:

# Root configuration
agents:
  - name: "fullstack-architect"
    system_prompt: |
      For domain-specific tasks, delegate to specialized agents:

      **Backend Tasks**: Use backend-engineer agent for:
      - API endpoint implementation
      - Database schema changes
      - Authentication middleware

      **Frontend Tasks**: Use frontend-engineer agent for:
      - React component development
      - State management implementation
      - User interface design

      **Infrastructure Tasks**: Use devsecops-engineer agent for:
      - CI/CD pipeline changes
      - Container configuration  
      - Security reviews

docs.title: 4. Output Organization
docs.text:
Organize outputs to match your project structure:

# Root ai-rulez.yaml
outputs:
  - file: "CLAUDE.md"                    # System overview
  - path: ".cursor/rules/"
    type: "rule"
    naming_scheme: "system.mdc"

# backend/ai-rulez.yaml  
outputs:
  - file: "CLAUDE.md"                    # Backend-specific
  - path: "../.cursor/rules/"
    type: "rule"
    naming_scheme: "backend.mdc"

# frontend/ai-rulez.yaml
outputs:
  - file: "CLAUDE.md"                    # Frontend-specific
  - path: "../.cursor/rules/"
    type: "rule" 
    naming_scheme: "frontend.mdc"

This creates: 

.cursor/rules/
├── system.mdc      # Root rules
├── backend.mdc     # Backend rules  
└── frontend.mdc    # Frontend rules

docs.title: 5. Rule Priority Management
docs.text:
Use priority levels to create rule hierarchy:

  • 100-90: System architecture and cross-cutting concerns (root)
  • 89-80: Technology stack patterns (scoped configs)
  • 79-70: Specific implementation details (scoped configs)
  • 69-50: Code style and conventions (scoped configs)

docs.title: 6. Shared Configurations with Remote Includes
docs.text:
Share common patterns across projects:

# Root configuration
includes:
  - "https://your-org.com/ai-rules/typescript-standards.yaml"
  - "https://your-org.com/ai-rules/security-patterns.yaml"
  - "./local-overrides.yaml"

# Backend-specific
includes:
  - "https://your-org.com/ai-rules/nodejs-api-patterns.yaml"
  - "../shared-backend-rules.yaml"

docs.title: File Discovery
docs.text:
ai-rulez automatically discovers configurations in subdirectories:

# From project root, finds and processes all configs
ai-rulez generate

# From specific directory, processes only that config
cd backend && ai-rulez generate

# Explicit config file
ai-rulez generate --config custom-config.yaml

docs.title: From Single File to Multiple Files
docs.text:
1. Start with existing configuration: 

    # Current ai-rulez.yaml (too large)
rules:
  - name: "Frontend Patterns" 
  - name: "Backend Patterns"
  - name: "Database Rules"
  # ... 50 more rules

  2. Extract by domain: 

    # Create domain-specific configs
mkdir -p backend frontend shared
# Move relevant rules to each file

  3. Update root to focus on architecture: 

    # New root ai-rulez.yaml
metadata:
  name: "Project Architecture"

rules:
  - name: "System Overview"
    priority: 100
    content: "High-level architecture and patterns"

agents:
  - name: "system-architect"
    # Define delegation patterns

  4. Test the split: 

    # Verify all files generate correctly
ai-rulez generate
ai-rulez validate

docs.title: Example 1: Value8 Cap Table Platform
docs.text:
value8/
├── ai-rulez.yaml           # System overview + agent definitions
├── backend/ai-rulez.yaml   # Fastify API patterns
└── apps/ai-rulez.yaml      # Next.js multi-zone architecture
  • Root: 13 rules, 548 lines - System architecture, agent delegation
  • Backend: 5 rules, 209 lines - Fastify patterns, database operations
  • Apps: 10 rules, 553 lines - Next.js components, authentication

docs.title: Example 2: GrantFlow AI Platform
docs.text:
grantflow/
└── ai-rulez.yaml           # Single file with specialized agents
  • Single file: 20 rules, 370 lines - All rules with agent specialization
  • Agents handle domain separation: python-engineer, typescript-engineer, devsecops-agent

docs.title: Pattern: Backend + Frontend Split
docs.text:
# Root: agents + system architecture
# backend/: API patterns, database, authentication  
# frontend/: components, routing, state management

docs.title: Pattern: Microservices
docs.text:
# Root: service communication, shared patterns
# service-*/: service-specific patterns and rules
# shared/: common utilities and types

docs.title: Pattern: Monolithic with Features
docs.text:
# Root: app-wide architecture and routing
# features/*/: feature-specific patterns
# shared/: reusable components and utilities

Choose the pattern that matches your project structure and team organization.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: Get started in 3 simple steps, then expand your configuration.

docs.title: 1. Create Your First Configuration
docs.text:
ai-rulez init "My Project"

This creates ai_rulez.yaml with a minimal Claude setup:

metadata:
  name: "My Project"

rules:
  - name: "code-quality"
    content: "Write clean, readable code"
    priority: 10

outputs:
  - path: "CLAUDE.md"

docs.title: 2. Generate AI Files
docs.text:
ai-rulez generate

Creates CLAUDE.md with your rules.

docs.title: 3. Validate Configuration
docs.text: ai-rulez validate

docs.title: Add More Rules
docs.text:
Edit ai_rulez.yaml:

metadata:
  name: "My Project"

rules:
  - name: "code-quality"
    content: "Write clean, readable code"
    priority: 10

  - name: "testing"
    content: "Write unit tests for all functions"
    priority: 9

outputs:
  - path: "CLAUDE.md"

docs.title: Add Multiple AI Platforms
docs.text:
# Add popular platforms
ai-rulez init "My Project" --popular

Or add specific platforms:

outputs:
  - path: "CLAUDE.md"
  - path: ".cursor/rules/rules.mdc"
  - path: ".github/copilot-instructions.md"

docs.title: Use CLI Commands
docs.text:
# Add a new rule
ai-rulez add rule "error-handling" --content "Handle all errors explicitly" --priority 8

# Add an output  
ai-rulez add output --path ".windsurf/rules.md"

docs.title: Advanced: Add Agents (Claude)
docs.text:
For specialized sub-tasks:

ai-rulez add agent "code-reviewer" \
  --description "Reviews code quality" \
  --system-prompt "You are a senior code reviewer"

docs.title: Git Hooks (Optional)
docs.text:
Automatically validate configuration on git commit:

ai-rulez init "My Project" --setup-hooks

Works with lefthook, pre-commit, or husky.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Learn about Configuration
  • Explore CLI Commands
  • See More Examples

docs.title: Configuration Schema
docs.text: ai-rulez uses JSON Schema to validate configuration files and provide editor support.

docs.title: Schema URL
docs.text:
$schema: "https://github.com/Goldziher/ai-rulez/schema/ai-rules-v1.schema.json"

Add this to your ai_rulez.yaml for editor autocompletion and validation:

$schema: "https://github.com/Goldziher/ai-rulez/schema/ai-rules-v1.schema.json"
metadata:
  name: "My Project"
rules:
  - name: "example"
    content: "Example rule"
outputs:
  - path: "CLAUDE.md"

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text:
• metadata.name - Project name (minimum 1 character)
  • outputs - At least one output configuration
  • Each rule must have name and content
  • Each section must have title and content
  • Each agent must have name and description

docs.title: Field Constraints
docs.text:
• Version: Must follow semantic versioning (e.g., 1.0.0)
  • Priority: Integer ≥ 1 (defaults to 1)
  • Agent names: Must be lowercase with hyphens only (^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$)
  • Includes: Must be valid HTTP/HTTPS URLs or local file paths
  • Output paths: Must have either path or file field (prefer path)

docs.title: Advanced Features
docs.text:
• Targets: Glob patterns for applying rules/sections to specific outputs
  • Templates: Built-in names, file references (@file.tmpl), or inline templates
  • User Rules: Personal overrides in user_rulez section

docs.title: Complete Schema Reference
docs.text: The full JSON Schema is available at: schema/ai-rules-v1.schema.json

docs.title: Schema Features
docs.text:
• ✅ Complete validation for all configuration fields
  • ✅ Editor autocompletion support (VS Code, IntelliJ, etc.)
  • ✅ Detailed error messages with field descriptions
  • ✅ Examples and constraints for each field
  • ✅ Support for remote includes validation
  • ✅ Template validation (built-in, file references, inline)

docs.title: Editor Setup
docs.text:
Most modern editors automatically detect JSON Schema from the $schema field. For manual setup:

VS Code: Install the YAML extension by Red Hat IntelliJ/WebStorm: Built-in YAML support with schema detection Vim/Neovim: Use coc-yaml or similar LSP plugins

docs.title: Valid Configuration
docs.text:
$schema: "https://github.com/Goldziher/ai-rulez/schema/ai-rules-v1.schema.json"
metadata:
  name: "My Project"
  version: "1.0.0"
rules:
  - name: "code-quality"
    content: "Write clean code"
    priority: 10
outputs:
  - path: "CLAUDE.md"

docs.title: Common Validation Errors
docs.text:
# ❌ Missing required name
metadata: {}

# ❌ Invalid version format  
metadata:
  name: "Project"
  version: "1.0"  # Should be "1.0.0"

# ❌ Invalid agent name
agents:
  - name: "Code_Reviewer"  # Should be "code-reviewer"
    description: "Reviews code"

# ❌ Missing outputs
metadata:
  name: "Project"
# outputs: []  # At least one output required

Use ai-rulez validate to check your configuration against the schema.
